application_credentials.description:
Follow the [instructions]({more_info_url}) to configure Smartcar for your vehicle:

1. Go to [Applications]({oauth_creds_url}) and your application from the list.
1. Choose **Configuration** from the side menu.
1. Copy the **Client ID**.
1. Regenerate and copy the the **Client Secret**.
1. Add `{redirect_url}` under *Authorized redirect URI*.

create_entry.default:
To send events to Home Assistant, you will need to set up a [webhook with Smartcar]({smartcar_url}). Use the following URL:

`{webhook_url}`

See [the documentation]({docs_url}) for further details.

error.no_scopes: At least one scope needs to be selected
error.extraneous_management_token: Do not provide application management token unless using webhooks
error.invalid_auth_code: The authorization code was rejected by Smartcar (expired, already used, or invalid). Generate a new one via the mobile SDK and paste it here.
error.oauth_failed: Smartcar's token endpoint returned an unexpected error. Check Home Assistant logs for details.
error.fast_must_be_le_base: The fast polling interval must be less than or equal to the base interval.

reauth_confirm.description:
The Smartcar integration needs to re-authenticate your account.

Choose **OAuth re-authentication** for the standard browser-based flow.

Choose **Manual token entry** if the OAuth flow is unavailable for your vehicle (for example, BMW vehicles in the US since 2025-09 require Smartcar's mobile SDK and cannot complete the web OAuth flow). You will need an `access_token` + `refresh_token` minted out-of-band via the mobile SDK.
reauth_confirm.title: Authentication expired for {name}

manual_token.description:
Paste the **authorization code** from Smartcar's mobile SDK (the value returned to your `sc<client_id>://exchange` callback). The integration will exchange it for an access + refresh token using the configured application credentials, then validate by listing your vehicles.

Auth codes are single-use and short-lived (~10 minutes). If exchange fails, run the mobile pairing again to get a fresh code.

After exchange, Home Assistant takes over — the refresh token rotates automatically on each access-token expiry (~2h) and stays valid for 60 days from last use.
manual_token.title: Manual token entry

data.application_management_token: Token: only provide if using webhooks
data.fast_scan_interval_minutes: Fast polling while charging or active (minutes, 30–720, must be ≤ base)

webhooks.description:
Provide your _Application Management Token_ from your [Smartcar account]({smartcar_url}) to use webhooks.

**Polling:** the integration polls Smartcar at the base interval (default 6h). When a vehicle is charging or actively awake-and-plugged-in, it polls at the fast interval (default 1h). When asleep and unplugged, it slows to 2× the base interval. Free tier is 500 API calls/month/vehicle — defaults use ~120/month, leaving budget for `smartcar.poll_now` automations.

See [the documentation]({docs_url}) for further details.
webhooks.title: Smartcar configuration

data.read_battery: Get EV/PHEV battery level, capacity & current range
data.read_charge: Get details on whether the car is plugged in and charging
data.read_security: Get details on whether doors, windows & more are enabled

webhooks.description:
Provide your _Application Management Token_ from your [Smartcar account]({smartcar_url}) to use webhooks.

To send events to Home Assistant, you will need to set up a [webhook with Smartcar]({smartcar_url}). Use the following URL:

`{webhook_url}`

If the above URL indicates that webhooks are not enabled, revisit this configuration screen after completing the setup to get the URL.

**Polling:** the integration polls Smartcar at the base interval (default 6h). When a vehicle is charging or plugged in, it polls at the fast interval (default 1h). Free tier is 500 API calls/month/vehicle. The `smartcar.poll_now` service forces an immediate refresh from automations (debounced 60s).

See [the documentation]({docs_url}) for further details.
webhooks.title: Smartcar configuration

poll_now.description: Force an immediate refresh of vehicle data, bypassing the polling interval. Calls are debounced (60s) so a noisy automation cannot drain the API budget.

vin.description: Optional. Refresh only this VIN; otherwise refreshes all vehicles in the entry.
vin.name: VIN

poll_now.name: Refresh vehicle data now
